methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $270M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
REGENT Craft is a Series A-stage transportation company founded by Billy Thalheimer, currently valued at $270M post-money with $100M+ raised to date. The transportation sector comp universe includes 8 private peers (Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, Pipedream among them) and no public comparables, limiting direct peer benchmarking.

The valuation analysis applies one quantitative method. Stage-progression modeling, which is applicable here, uses a 2.0x step-up multiplier from the $270M Series A post-money baseline to estimate next-stage entry valuation, yielding $540M. Peer-median analysis is not applicable due to insufficient narrow-stage comparable data—while 4 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 met same-stage or adjacent-stage filtering criteria, making sector median benchmarking unreliable.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $459M to $621M, with a median of $540M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity band around the stage-progression estimate. The primary uncertainty is the absence of relevant same-stage peer multiples; the analysis relies on a single methodological input rather than cross-validation across multiple approaches. Further clarity on comparable company selection, near-term milestones, and capital deployment pace would strengthen conviction in this range.
